style: Approved SleepRadar pilot shell: calm technical night UI, solid low-glare surfaces, restrained semantic color, no authored gradients, and one finite radar activation motif.

rules.application: Three concentric solid-border rings make one finite outward relay from the canonical mark; no gradient, repeat, yoyo, or persistent shimmer.
Heart rate then Breathing move only eight pixels and scale from 0.985 to 1 with power3.out; no overshoot.

layout.hero: canonical mark centered above two 608px Home Assistant sensor cards

truth_guards: Both semantic colors are paired with visible units and the word measured.
Unknown states stay neutral and do not invent an empty-bed reading.
No diagnosis, clinical precision, medical framing, or safety claim.

timeline.action: Hold the readable hook, subdued mark, and two neutral unknown sensor cards exactly still.
Begin one finite solid-border radar pulse and resolve Heart rate to bpm and measured with a red label accent.
Resolve Breathing to br/min and measured with a blue label accent; settle the final status dot by 3.0 seconds.
Hold both activated cards fully readable with no ambient loop.
Reverse Breathing then Heart rate and restore the subdued neutral opening state.
Hold the exact opening CSS state for the final tenth of a second.

scenes.motion: none; fully readable at frame zero

scenes.text: Heart rate · bpm · measured. Breathing · br/min · measured.
scenes.motion: finite radar pulse plus ordered smooth card activation

scenes.text: Heart rate and breathing measured contact-free.

scenes.motion: exact inverse activation then final opening-state hold
